name: Policy Impact Analyzer
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: GPT-4o for regulatory analysis — change detection summarization, cross-sector impact assessment, stakeholder mapping, compliance gap narratives, policy comparison reports

prod.note: ~1.2M tokens/day, gpt-4o for complex legal analysis

services.name: Azure AI Search
services.purpose: Semantic search over regulatory corpus — federal/state/local regulations, proposed rules, enforcement actions, industry guidance, historical policy amendments

prod.note: 100GB storage, semantic ranker, large regulatory corpus

services.name: Azure Document Intelligence
services.purpose: Extract structured data from regulatory documents — PDF rule filings, Federal Register notices, legislative bill text, amendment redlines, compliance checklists

services.name: Cosmos DB
services.purpose: Regulatory change records, impact assessments, stakeholder maps, compliance tracking, policy version history, cross-reference graph

prod.note: Dedicated throughput, change feed for event processing

enterprise.note: Multi-region, analytical store for trend analysis

services.name: Azure Functions
services.purpose: Regulatory feed ingestion (Federal Register, state gazettes), change detection pipeline, impact scoring triggers, stakeholder notification dispatch, scheduled compliance scans

services.name: Container Apps
services.purpose: Policy analysis API — impact assessment engine, stakeholder mapping, compliance gap tracker, report generator, regulatory dashboard backend

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: Regulatory feed API keys, government database credentials, client data encryption keys, notification service secrets

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Change detection latency, impact assessment accuracy, regulatory feed health, API throughput, analysis pipeline timing

optimization_tips: Use GPT-4o-mini for initial change detection triage and basic summarization — only escalate to GPT-4o for complex cross-sector impact analysis and stakeholder mapping, saving 65% on AI costs
Implement incremental indexing for AI Search — only re-index changed or new regulatory documents rather than full corpus rebuilds, reducing search ingestion costs by 70%
Cache Document Intelligence extraction results — regulatory documents are immutable once published, so extracted structures can be permanently cached after first processing
Use Cosmos DB change feed to trigger impact assessments reactively — only analyze when new regulations appear rather than polling all sources continuously

Schedule non-urgent analysis (historical trend reports, cross-sector correlation studies) during off-peak hours using Functions consumption plan — regulatory analysis is rarely time-critical beyond initial change detection
Set Azure Cost Management budgets with alerts at 50%, 80%, 100%
